cards.back: A type of encryption where the same key is used for both encryption and decryption
A type of encryption where a pair of keys is used, one for encryption and another for decryption
A one-way process that takes input data of any size and produces a fixed-size string of characters, often used for data integrity and authenticity verification
A document that contains public-key information and is used to establish trust and authenticity between two parties
A type of encryption that divides the data into fixed-length blocks and encrypts each block separately
